title: carex vesicaria subsp. subfusca
intro: Carex vesicaria subsp. subfusca, also known as the blister sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial sedge that is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex vesicaria subsp. subfusca include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in salves and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
